message:
test: close codex install bats coverage

Close git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.

- validated tests/install/agent_config_test.bats with 48 passing Bats cases

- isolate common failure artifacts by CARGO_TARGET_DIR and test binary to avoid nextest collisions across reused tests/common modules

Verification: bats tests/install/agent_config_test.bats; cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_dcg_cod4 cargo test --test history_full_flow common::logging::tests::save_failure_artifact_writes_sanitized_path -- --exact --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_dcg_cod4 cargo test --test history_integration common::logging::tests::save_failure_artifact_writes_sanitized_path -- --exact --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_dcg_cod4 cargo nextest run --workspace; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_dcg_cod4 cargo check --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_dcg_cod4 cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings

files.patch:
@@ -588,7 +588,7 @@
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.2", "title": "[P1.2] Unit test: output_denial_for_protocol(Codex) writes empty stdout, non-empty stderr", "description": "**What this proves:** The new `HookProtocol::Codex` match arm at src/hook.rs:862 actually does what its comment claims: no stdout JSON, stderr already contains the colored deny message from `print_colorful_warning`. This is currently dead-line untested code from a coverage perspective.

**Test inputs:**
- A representative deny scenario: command = `git reset --hard HEAD~1`, pack = "core.git", pattern = "reset-hard", explanation = the canned text, allow_once = Some(...), severity = critical.

**Assertions:**
- stdout buffer: `is_empty()` (NOT just whitespace — strictly zero bytes; codex's parser at codex-rs/hooks/src/events/pre_tool_use.rs:165 trims and treats empty as the silent-allow path)
- stderr buffer: non-empty AND contains "BLOCKED" AND contains the exact command string
- stderr buffer: contains the rule id `core.git:reset-hard` (codex's parser uses stderr verbatim as the block reason — agents need this signal)

**Anti-pattern to avoid:** asserting on the exact byte sequence of the colored output. The test should check substrings, not bytes, so theme/palette tweaks don't cause spurious failures.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.066542268Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T04:01:00.947604216Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T04:01:00.947072342Z", "close_reason": "Unit test test_write_denial_codex_produces_empty_stdout covers all acceptance criteria: stdout strictly empty (zero bytes), stderr non-empty, stderr contains the command string and rule id core.git:reset-hard.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "unit-tests"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.066542268Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.066542268Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.3", "title": "[P1.3] Unit test: output_warning_for_protocol(Codex) writes empty stdout, stderr warns", "description": "**What this proves:** The Codex no-op arm in `output_warning_for_protocol` (src/hook.rs:1050) preserves the stderr warning emitted before the match while writing zero bytes to stdout. Codex's parser would reject any JSON we emit with our ergonomic fields, and an "ask" decision it can't parse is worse than no JSON at all.

**Inputs:**
- warn-severity command, pack = some warn-eligible pack, explanation present.

**Assertions:**
- stdout: 0 bytes
- stderr: contains "WARNING:" and the rule id
- stderr: does NOT contain "BLOCKED" (warn != deny)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.278191911Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T04:01:41.872991233Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T04:01:41.872591115Z", "close_reason": "test_write_warning_codex_produces_empty_stdout asserts: stdout 0 bytes, stderr contains WARNING marker and rule id core.git:checkout-dot.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "unit-tests"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.3", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.278191911Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.3", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.278191911Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.4", "title": "[P1.4] Unit tests: detect_protocol negative-space coverage", "description": "**What this proves:** The Codex disambiguator at src/hook.rs:378-382 only fires for the precise (Bash | launch-process) + non-empty turn_id combination. A future refactor that loosens either side would silently mis-classify and we want regression coverage now.

**Cases to add as `#[test]` blocks in src/hook.rs (each named `test_detect_protocol_*` for grep-ability):**
1. `tool_name = "Read"` + turn_id present  -> ClaudeCompatible (no shell tool, dcg should not engage anyway, but protocol classification should not flip to Codex)
2. `tool_name = "Bash"` + turn_id absent + tool_use_id present -> ClaudeCompatible (already covered as test_claude_code_with_tool_use_id_is_not_codex; verify still passes)
3. `tool_name = "launch-process"` + non-empty turn_id  -> Codex (sister tool name to Bash; rare but supported)
4. `tool_name = "Bash"` + turn_id = "   " (whitespace only)  -> ClaudeCompatible (defense against weird clients; the existing check `is_some_and(|s| !s.is_empty())` does NOT trim, document this trade-off in the test as a comment)
5. `tool_name = "BASH"` (uppercase) + non-empty turn_id  -> Codex (detect_protocol lowercases tool_name before comparison; this guards against future case-handling regressions)
6. `tool_name = "bash"` (lowercase already) + non-empty turn_id  -> Codex (parity for the lowercase wire form)
7. Copilot-shaped payload (event field present) + turn_id  -> Copilot (event field check fires first at src/hook.rs:364, codex check is later; verifies precedence ordering)
8. Gemini-shaped payload (run_shell_command + BeforeTool envelope) + turn_id  -> Gemini (turn_id is supposed to be Codex-only, but if some weird future Gemini variant adopts it we want classification to follow the stronger Gemini envelope signal)

**Acceptance:** All 8 tests pass. Tests use `serde_json::from_str` (no literal HookInput construction) so adding fields to HookInput doesn't break them. Each test includes a one-line doc comment explaining what regression it catches.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.488334221Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T04:02:49.527814167Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T04:02:49.527392719Z", "close_reason": "Added 8 detect_protocol negative-space tests: non-shell with turn_id, launch-process Codex, whitespace turn_id, uppercase BASH, lowercase bash, Copilot event override, Gemini envelope override, tool_use_id without turn_id. All pass.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "unit-tests"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1.4", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:53:01.488334221Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
-{"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.2", "title": "Phase 4: Install/uninstall correctness for codex (bats)", "description": "**Goal:** Provable install→uninstall round trip for the codex hook configuration. Today `tests/install/agent_config_test.bats:619/639/653` assert `CODEX_STATUS=unsupported`, which is stale and predates the real configure_codex implementation. The new uninstall_codex (added to uninstall.sh in this same effort) has zero bats coverage.

**Why this matters:** install.sh and uninstall.sh both edit `~/.codex/hooks.json` as JSON. A merge-mistake there could either (a) silently disable dcg under codex, or (b) corrupt unrelated hooks (e.g., the user's atuin entry). Bats round-trip tests are the only existing harness that exercises these shell functions in isolation.

**Approach:**
- Fix the 3 stale assertions in agent_config_test.bats (Phase-4.1)
- Add new bats tests for the unconfigure_codex code paths added to uninstall.sh in commit <new>
- Mirror the synthetic test scenarios used in /tmp/test_uninstall_codex.sh (already proven correct)

**Acceptance:**
- `bats tests/install/agent_config_test.bats` passes
- Round-trip test: install.sh → uninstall.sh leaves zero dcg artifacts in `~/.codex/`
- Coexistence test: install.sh into a hooks.json that already has atuin's PostToolUse → install adds dcg PreToolUse without touching atuin; uninstall removes only dcg", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:51:51.511682303Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-27T23:51:51.511682303Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["codex", "coverage", "installer"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.2", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:51:51.511682303Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2","title":"Phase 4: Install/uninstall correctness for codex (bats)","description":"**Goal:** Provable install→uninstall round trip for the codex hook configuration. Today `tests/install/agent_config_test.bats:619/639/653` assert `CODEX_STATUS=unsupported`, which is stale and predates the real configure_codex implementation. The new uninstall_codex (added to uninstall.sh in this same effort) has zero bats coverage.

**Why this matters:** install.sh and uninstall.sh both edit `~/.codex/hooks.json` as JSON. A merge-mistake there could either (a) silently disable dcg under codex, or (b) corrupt unrelated hooks (e.g., the user's atuin entry). Bats round-trip tests are the only existing harness that exercises these shell functions in isolation.

**Approach:**
- Fix the 3 stale assertions in agent_config_test.bats (Phase-4.1)
- Add new bats tests for the unconfigure_codex code paths added to uninstall.sh in commit <new>
- Mirror the synthetic test scenarios used in /tmp/test_uninstall_codex.sh (already proven correct)

**Acceptance:**
- `bats tests/install/agent_config_test.bats` passes
- Round-trip test: install.sh → uninstall.sh leaves zero dcg artifacts in `~/.codex/`
- Coexistence test: install.sh into a hooks.json that already has atuin's PostToolUse → install adds dcg PreToolUse without touching atuin; uninstall removes only dcg","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:51:51.511682303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:34:39.494492678Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T08:34:39.493968125Z","close_reason":"Validated Codex install/uninstall Bats coverage and fixed shared artifact path nextest isolation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","coverage","installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:51:51.511682303Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.1", "title": "[P4.1] Fix stale CODEX_STATUS=unsupported assertions in agent_config_test.bats", "description": "**The stale tests:**
- `tests/install/agent_config_test.bats:619` (`configure_codex: detects via .codex directory`)
- `tests/install/agent_config_test.bats:639` (`configure_codex: detects via codex command`)
- `tests/install/agent_config_test.bats:653` (`configure_codex: reports unsupported (no pre-execution hooks)`)

All three assert `[ "$CODEX_STATUS" = "unsupported" ]`. The actual `configure_codex` in install.sh:1464 has not produced "unsupported" since it was rewritten to actually configure hooks.json. Today it sets one of: `created`, `merged`, `already`, `skipped`, `failed`.

**What to change:**
- Test 1 (.codex directory exists, hooks.json absent): expect `CODEX_STATUS=created` AND assert `~/.codex/hooks.json` exists with the dcg PreToolUse Bash matcher
- Test 2 (codex on PATH, .codex directory absent): expect `CODEX_STATUS=created` AND `~/.codex/hooks.json` is created (configure_codex creates the directory)
- Test 3 (re-run after Test 1 ran): expect `CODEX_STATUS=already` (idempotent re-run with dcg already present)
- Add a new test: existing hooks.json with non-dcg entries -> `CODEX_STATUS=merged` AND existing entries preserved AND dcg added at the front
- Add a new test: existing hooks.json contains dcg pointing at a STALE path -> install updates the path (the merge logic at install.sh:1551-1563 should overwrite the dcg command entry)
- Add a new test: python3 unavailable + existing hooks.json -> `CODEX_STATUS=failed`, backup is restored, no partial-write damage

**Detailed logging:** each bats test logs `log_test "configure_codex variant: <name>"` and on failure runs `cat ~/.codex/hooks.json` to its log so postmortem is one click away.

**Why P1 (not P0):** these are pre-existing stale assertions, not regressions from this work. But fixing them is in scope because the docs comment block we updated in install.sh now claims behavior that contradicts these stale assertions. Leaving them stale invites confusion.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:57:17.147119090Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T04:04:52.729632650Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T04:04:52.729219327Z", "close_reason": "Completed by commit 76d1564: fixed Codex installer bats assertions, added created/already/merged/stale-path/python3-missing coverage, and updated configure_codex to refresh stale dcg hook paths. Verified focused bats and Rust tests; filed git_safety_guard-0rqa for unrelated e2e warn/ask mismatch.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["bats", "codex", "coverage", "installer"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.1", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-ovw4.2", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-04-27T23:57:17.147119090Z", "created_by": "ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.2","title":"[P4.2] Add bats tests for unconfigure_codex (six scenarios)","description":"**Goal:** Translate the synthetic /tmp/test_uninstall_codex.sh scenarios I ran during the second review pass into committed bats tests under `tests/install/agent_config_test.bats`. Plus extend with degraded-path scenarios.

**Test cases to add (each gets its own @test block):**

**Happy path:**
1. `unconfigure_codex: deletes hooks.json when only dcg is present`
2. `unconfigure_codex: preserves coexisting atuin hook in same Bash matcher`
3. `unconfigure_codex: preserves separate matcher block for atuin (^Bash$)`
4. `unconfigure_codex: preserves PostToolUse when only PreToolUse had dcg`
5. `unconfigure_codex: no-op when file has no dcg entries`
6. `unconfigure_codex: no-op when file does not exist`

**Degraded-path:**
7. `unconfigure_codex: malformed JSON -> no-op, file unchanged` (bats setup writes `{not valid json}` and asserts the function doesn't damage it)
8. `unconfigure_codex: PreToolUse is not a list -> no-op, file unchanged`
9. `unconfigure_codex: hooks key is not a dict -> no-op, file unchanged`
10. `unconfigure_codex: python3 unavailable -> returns 1, prints warning, hooks.json unchanged` (bats hides python3 by prepending an empty PATH)
11. `unconfigure_codex: hooks.json read-only directory -> returns 1, prints warning` (chmod the dir; cleanup must restore perms)
12. `unconfigure_codex: hooks.json points at a "dcg-helper" command (substring match concern) -> dcg removed, dcg-helper PRESERVED` (regression test for the substring-match concern flagged in the original review)

**Setup helpers needed in test_helper.bash:**
- `seed_codex_hooks_json <json_string>` -> writes the JSON to mock $HOME/.codex/hooks.json
- `assert_codex_hooks_deleted` -> asserts the file is gone
- `assert_codex_hooks_contains <substring>` -> grep -q wrapper
- `assert_codex_hooks_not_contains <substring>`
- `assert_codex_hooks_unchanged` -> compares against a saved copy

**Detailed logging:** each test logs the pre-state JSON (or absence) and post-state JSON via `log_test` so a CI failure includes the exact transition.

**Why P1:** These are direct coverage of code I added in this work (uninstall.sh:337-424). Nothing else exercises that code path automatically.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:57:17.400125985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T07:55:38.643586284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T07:55:38.643191464Z","close_reason":"Added Codex uninstall Bats coverage for unconfigure_codex happy/degraded paths and fixed exact dcg command matching","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bats","codex","coverage","installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:57:17.400125985Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.3","title":"[P4.3] Round-trip: install + uninstall + repeat (idempotent)","description":"**Goal:** Round-trip test that the install/uninstall pair leaves zero artifacts when applied to a clean codex setup, AND preserves coexisting non-dcg hooks under realistic conditions.

**Scenario A (clean install/uninstall round trip):**
1. Mock home with empty `~/.codex/` directory and a mock `codex` binary on PATH
2. Run `configure_codex` -> assert hooks.json exists with dcg, CODEX_STATUS=created
3. Run `unconfigure_codex` -> assert hooks.json is gone (file deleted because dcg was its only content)
4. Re-run `configure_codex` -> idempotent: same as step 2
5. Re-run `unconfigure_codex` -> idempotent: same as step 3
6. Re-run `unconfigure_codex` AGAIN with no file present -> idempotent: still exit 0

**Scenario B (coexistence with atuin):**
1. Mock home with `~/.codex/hooks.json` containing only an atuin PostToolUse `^Bash$` entry (the typical atuin install shape)
2. Run `configure_codex` -> CODEX_STATUS=merged; assert dcg added; atuin entry byte-identical
3. Run `unconfigure_codex` -> assert dcg gone; atuin entry byte-identical; file kept

**Scenario C (double install):**
1. Empty home, run `configure_codex` -> created
2. Run `configure_codex` again -> already (NOT created, NOT merged)
3. Hooks.json has exactly ONE dcg entry (no duplicates)

**Scenario D (stale dcg path replaced):**
1. Seed hooks.json with `command: "/old/path/dcg"`
2. Run `configure_codex` with current dcg at `/new/path/dcg`
3. Hooks.json now has `command: "/new/path/dcg"` (the install.sh merge logic at lines 1551-1563 should overwrite stale entries)
4. Run `unconfigure_codex` -> file deleted

**Scenario E (corrupt round trip):**
1. Empty home, run `configure_codex`
2. Manually corrupt `~/.codex/hooks.json` mid-install (e.g., truncate)
3. Run `unconfigure_codex` -> no panic; file either left as-is or deleted; no exception trail in stderr

**Detailed logging:** each scenario logs the JSON state at each step. On failure, the log includes the exact diff between expected and actual state.

**Why this matters more than the unit-level Phase 4.2 cases:** Round-trip checks the install/uninstall functions interact correctly. If install adds a key uninstall doesn't know to remove, this catches it. If uninstall accidentally deletes coexisting entries, this catches it. These are integration-level guarantees.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:57:17.664636839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:21:15.087904138Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T08:21:15.087500923Z","close_reason":"Added Codex install/uninstall round-trip coverage in a93af78.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bats","codex","coverage","installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:57:17.664636839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:57:17.664636839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ovw4.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:57:17.664636839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -736,7 +736,7 @@
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ujs7", "title": "Treat ag/ack as search commands in sanitizer", "description": "sanitize_for_pattern_matching should treat ag/ack like rg/grep so positional patterns are masked and -e/--pattern flags set search_pattern_masked correctly to avoid false positives.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-01-13T01:18:49.078164063Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-01-13T01:21:16.654544298Z", "closed_at": "2026-01-13T01:21:16.654544298Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0}
 {"id": "git_safety_guard-ulmv", "title": "Command Telemetry: Export options (JSON, CSV)", "notes": "
---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_json_export_format() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_data(10);
    let mut buf = Vec::new();

    db.export_json(&mut buf, &ExportOptions::default()).unwrap();

    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&buf).unwrap();
    assert!(json["exported_at"].is_string());
    assert!(json["total_records"].as_i64().unwrap() >= 10);
    assert!(json["commands"].is_array());
}

#[test]
fn test_csv_export_format() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_data(10);
    let mut buf = Vec::new();

    db.export_csv(&mut buf, &ExportOptions::default()).unwrap();

    let content = String::from_utf8(buf).unwrap();
    // Should have header row
    assert!(content.starts_with("id,timestamp,"));
    // Should have data rows
    assert!(content.lines().count() >= 11);  // header + 10 data
}

#[test]
fn test_jsonl_export_streaming() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_data(1000);
    let mut buf = Vec::new();

    db.export_jsonl(&mut buf, &ExportOptions::default()).unwrap();

    let content = String::from_utf8(buf).unwrap();
    // Each line should be valid JSON
    for line in content.lines() {
        serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).unwrap();
    }
    assert_eq!(content.lines().count(), 1000);
}

#[test]
fn test_export_with_filters() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_mixed_outcomes(100);
    let mut buf = Vec::new();

    db.export_json(&mut buf, &ExportOptions {
        outcome_filter: Some(Outcome::Deny),
        ..Default::default()
    }).unwrap();

    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&buf).unwrap();
    for cmd in json["commands"].as_array().unwrap() {
        assert_eq!(cmd["outcome"], "deny");
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_large_export_streaming() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_data(100_000);

    // Should not OOM - verify streaming works
    let mut counter = std::io::sink();  // Discard output
    db.export_jsonl(&mut counter, &ExportOptions::default()).unwrap();
}

#[test]
fn test_export_with_date_range() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_date_range();
    let mut buf = Vec::new();

    db.export_json(&mut buf, &ExportOptions {
        since: Some(Utc::now() - Duration::days(7)),
        until: Some(Utc::now()),
        ..Default::default()
    }).unwrap();

    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&buf).unwrap();
    // Should only include recent entries
    assert!(json["total_records"].as_i64().unwrap() < 100);
}

#[test]
fn test_compressed_export() {
    let db = create_test_db_with_data(1000);
    let mut buf = Vec::new();

    db.export_json_compressed(&mut buf, &ExportOptions::default()).unwrap();

    // Should be gzip compressed
    assert_eq!(&buf[0..2], &[0x1f, 0x8b]);  // Gzip magic bytes
}
```

### E2E Tests

```bash
# Test JSON export
dcg telemetry export --format json --output /tmp/export.json
jq empty /tmp/export.json && echo "PASS: Valid JSON"

# Test CSV export
dcg telemetry export --format csv --output /tmp/export.csv
head -1 /tmp/export.csv | grep -q "id,timestamp" && echo "PASS: CSV header"

# Test JSONL export
dcg telemetry export --format jsonl --output /tmp/export.jsonl
head -1 /tmp/export.jsonl | jq empty && echo "PASS: Valid JSONL"

# Test filtered export
dcg telemetry export --outcome deny --format json --output /tmp/blocks.json
jq '.commands | all(.outcome == "deny")' /tmp/blocks.json | grep -q true && echo "PASS: Filter works"

# Test compressed export
dcg telemetry export --compress --output /tmp/export.json.gz
file /tmp/export.json.gz | grep -q gzip && echo "PASS: Compression works"

 {"id": "git_safety_guard-unit", "title": "Create comprehensive unit test suite for heredoc detection", "description": "# Unit Test Suite for Heredoc Detection

## Objective

Create thorough unit tests for each component of the heredoc detection system.

## Test Modules

### 1. Tier 1 Regex Tests (`tests/tier1_regex.rs`)

```rust
mod tier1_regex_tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_bash_heredoc_variants() {
        // Standard heredoc
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat << EOF"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<EOF"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat << 'EOF'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat << \"EOF\""));
        
        // Indented heredoc
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<- EOF"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<-EOF"));
        
        // Here-string
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<< 'hello'"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_inline_script_flags() {
        // Python
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("python -c 'print(1)'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("python3 -c 'print(1)'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("python -e 'print(1)'")); // -e not valid but we catch
        
        // Node.js
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("node -e 'console.log(1)'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("node -p 'process.version'"));
        
        // Ruby
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("ruby -e 'puts 1'"));
        
        // Perl
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("perl -e 'print 1'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("perl -E 'say 1'"));
        
        // Shell
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("bash -c 'echo hi'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("sh -c 'echo hi'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("zsh -c 'echo hi'"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_pipe_to_interpreter() {
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("curl http://x.com | bash"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat script.py | python3"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("echo code | node"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_negative_cases() {
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("git status"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("cargo build"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("ls -la"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("cat file.txt"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("grep pattern file"));
    }
}
```

### 2. Heredoc Extraction Tests (`tests/extraction.rs`)

```rust
mod extraction_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_extract_simple_heredoc() {
        let cmd = "python3 << 'EOF'\nprint('hello')\nEOF";
        let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(heredoc.content, "print('hello')\n");
        assert_eq!(heredoc.delimiter, "EOF");
        assert!(heredoc.quoted);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_extract_indented_heredoc() {
        let cmd = "bash <<- 'END'\n\techo hello\n\techo world\n\tEND";
        let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd).unwrap();
        // <<- strips leading tabs
        assert_eq!(heredoc.content, "echo hello\necho world\n");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_extract_inline_script() {
        let cmd = "python3 -c 'import os; os.system(\"ls\")'";
        let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(heredoc.content, "import os; os.system(\"ls\")");
    }
}
```

### 3. Language Detection Tests (`tests/language_detection.rs`)

```rust
mod language_detection_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_detect_from_command() {
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("python3 -c"), Language::Python);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("node -e"), Language::JavaScript);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("ruby -e"), Language::Ruby);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("perl -e"), Language::Perl);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("bash -c"), Language::Bash);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_detect_from_shebang() {
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/usr/bin/env python3\n"), Language::Python);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/bin/bash\n"), Language::Bash);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/usr/bin/node\n"), Language::JavaScript);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_detect_from_content_heuristics() {
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("import os\n"), Language::Python);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("require('fs')\n"), Language::JavaScript);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("use strict;\n"), Language::Perl);
    }
}
```

### 4. Pattern Matching Tests (`tests/patterns.rs`)

One submodule per language with comprehensive tests.

### 5. False Positive Tests (`tests/false_positives.rs`)

```rust
mod false_positive_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_documentation_allowed() {
        // These should NOT be blocked
        assert!(check_command("bd create --description='blocks rm -rf'").is_allowed());
        assert!(check_command("git commit -m 'fix rm -rf detection'").is_allowed());
        assert!(check_command("grep 'rm -rf' src/").is_allowed());
    }
}
```

## Coverage Requirements

- Line coverage: > 90%
- Branch coverage: > 85%
- All public functions have tests
- All error paths tested", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-01-08T01:00:00Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-01-08T02:15:47.843153995Z", "closed_at": "2026-01-08T02:15:47.843153995Z", "close_reason": "Duplicate; consolidated into git_safety_guard-8wt", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-unit", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-49s", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-01-08T00:28:33.346249627Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-unit", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-du4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-01-08T00:28:33.663971771Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-unit", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-t1rx", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-01-08T00:28:33.163885724Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "git_safety_guard-unit", "depends_on_id": "git_safety_guard-y4j", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-01-08T00:28:33.507729581Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "", "thread_id": ""}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-upo1","title":"Add Codex hook configuration to install.ps1 + create uninstall.ps1","description":"**Goal:** Bring install.ps1 to parity with install.sh for Codex hook configuration, and create uninstall.ps1.

**Audit findings (from ovw4.2.4):**

1. **install.ps1** has zero Codex hook configuration — only outputs a Claude Code settings.json snippet at the end
2. **No uninstall.ps1** exists
3. install.ps1 does not detect Codex (~/.codex/ dir or codex.exe on PATH)
4. install.ps1 does not write ~/.codex/hooks.json
5. No Windows path handling for the command field in hooks.json (backslash escaping for JSON)

**What install.sh does that install.ps1 should mirror:**

- `configure_codex()` (install.sh:1464-1670):
  - Detects codex via `command -v codex` or `~/.codex/` directory existence
  - Creates `~/.codex/hooks.json` with PreToolUse Bash matcher pointing at dcg binary
  - Merges into existing hooks.json preserving non-dcg hooks (atuin, etc.) via Python
  - Updates stale dcg paths on re-install
  - Reports status: created/merged/already/skipped/failed

- `unconfigure_codex()` (uninstall.sh:345-430):
  - Removes dcg entries from hooks.json preserving other hooks
  - Deletes hooks.json if dcg was the only entry
  - Handles missing/corrupt files gracefully

**Implementation notes:**

- PowerShell has native JSON support via `ConvertFrom-Json`/`ConvertTo-Json` — no Python dependency needed
- Windows path: `$HOME\.local\bin\dcg.exe` (or wherever -Dest points)
- hooks.json command field needs forward slashes or properly escaped backslashes
- uninstall.ps1 should handle: binary removal, Claude Code settings.json cleanup, Codex hooks.json cleanup, PATH removal

**Acceptance:**
- install.ps1 detects Codex and writes ~/.codex/hooks.json (same shape as install.sh)
- install.ps1 merges safely with existing hooks.json entries
- uninstall.ps1 exists and reverses both Claude Code and Codex configurations

 {"id": "git_safety_guard-v2ti", "title": "[E0-T1] Create comprehensive E2E test infrastructure", "description": "## Task: Create Comprehensive E2E Test Infrastructure

### Context
This is a CROSS-CUTTING task that provides the foundation for E2E testing across ALL 10 epics. Each epic's testing task will depend on this infrastructure.

### What To Do
Create a robust E2E test framework with detailed logging, fixture management, and reusable test utilities.

### Implementation Details

1. **Test Framework Structure:**
   ```
   tests/
   ├── e2e/
   │   ├── mod.rs                    # E2E test module
   │   ├── framework.rs              # Test framework utilities
   │   ├── logging.rs                # Detailed test logging
   │   ├── fixtures/
   │   │   ├── configs/              # Test config files
   │   │   │   ├── minimal.toml
   │   │   │   ├── full_featured.toml
   │   │   │   ├── path_specific.toml
   │   │   │   ├── temporary_rules.toml
   │   │   │   └── agent_profiles.toml
   │   │   ├── commands/             # Test command sets
   │   │   │   ├── dangerous.txt
   │   │   │   ├── safe.txt
   │   │   │   └── edge_cases.txt
   │   │   └── git_repos/            # Mock git repos for testing
   │   ├── test_pattern_testing.rs   # Epic 1 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_explanations.rs      # Epic 2 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_suggestions.rs       # Epic 3 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_auto_suggest.rs      # Epic 4 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_path_allowlist.rs    # Epic 5 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_temp_allowlist.rs    # Epic 6 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_interactive.rs       # Epic 7 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_git_awareness.rs     # Epic 8 E2E tests
   │   ├── test_agent_profiles.rs    # Epic 9 E2E tests
   │   └── test_graduated_response.rs # Epic 10 E2E tests
   └── scripts/
       ├── run_e2e_tests.sh          # Master E2E test runner
       ├── setup_test_env.sh         # Environment setup
       └── cleanup_test_env.sh       # Cleanup
   ```

2. **Test Framework Core (framework.rs):**
   ```rust
   pub struct E2ETestContext {
       pub temp_dir: TempDir,
       pub config_path: PathBuf,
       pub history_db: PathBuf,
       pub log_file: PathBuf,
       pub env_vars: HashMap<String, String>,
   }

   impl E2ETestContext {
       pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn with_config(self, config: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn with_git_repo(self, branch: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn with_agent(self, agent: &str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn run_dcg(&self, args: &[&str]) -> DcgOutput { ... }
       pub fn assert_blocked(&self, output: &DcgOutput) { ... }
       pub fn assert_allowed(&self, output: &DcgOutput) { ... }
       pub fn cleanup(self) { ... }
   }

   pub struct DcgOutput {
       pub stdout: String,
       pub stderr: String,
       pub exit_code: i32,
       pub duration_ms: u64,
   }
   ```

3. **Detailed Logging (logging.rs):**
   ```rust
   pub struct TestLogger {
       log_path: PathBuf,
       verbose: bool,
   }

   impl TestLogger {
       pub fn log_test_start(&self, name: &str, description: &str) { ... }
       pub fn log_step(&self, step: &str, details: &str) { ... }
       pub fn log_command(&self, cmd: &str, args: &[&str]) { ... }
       pub fn log_output(&self, output: &DcgOutput) { ... }
       pub fn log_assertion(&self, assertion: &str, passed: bool) { ... }
       pub fn log_test_end(&self, name: &str, result: TestResult) { ... }
       pub fn generate_report(&self) -> TestReport { ... }
   }
   ```

4. **E2E Test Script (run_e2e_tests.sh):**
   ```bash
   #!/bin/bash
   set -euo pipefail

   LOG_DIR="./test_logs/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
   mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

   echo "=== DCG E2E Test Suite ===" | tee "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
   echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"

   # Build release binary
   cargo build --release 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/build.log"

   # Run each test category
   for test in pattern_testing explanations suggestions auto_suggest \
               path_allowlist temp_allowlist interactive git_awareness \
               agent_profiles graduated_response; do
       echo "Running: test_${test}..." | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
       cargo test --test "test_${test}" -- --nocapture 2>&1 | \
           tee "$LOG_DIR/${test}.log"
       echo "  Result: $?" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
   done

   echo "Completed: $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/summary.log"
   echo "Logs saved to: $LOG_DIR"
   ```

### Fixture Requirements

1. **Config Fixtures:**
   - minimal.toml: Bare minimum config
   - full_featured.toml: All features enabled
   - path_specific.toml: Path-aware allowlists
   - temporary_rules.toml: TTL and session rules
   - agent_profiles.toml: Per-agent settings
   - git_awareness.toml: Branch-specific settings
   - graduated_response.toml: Response graduation config

2. **Command Fixtures:**
   - dangerous.txt: Commands that should be blocked
   - safe.txt: Commands that should be allowed
   - edge_cases.txt: Boundary conditions, unicode, special chars
   - heredocs.txt: Multiline/heredoc commands

3. **Mock Git Repos:**
   - Script to create temp git repos with specific branches
   - main, master, feature/*, release/* branches

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Test framework compiles and basic tests pass
- [ ] All fixture files created and validated
- [ ] run_e2e_tests.sh runs successfully
- [ ] Log files contain detailed, parseable output
- [ ] Test report generation works

### Dependencies
- None - this is foundational infrastructure

### Files to Create
- tests/e2e/mod.rs
- tests/e2e/framework.rs
- tests/e2e/logging.rs
- tests/e2e/fixtures/* (all fixture files)
- tests/scripts/run_e2e_tests.sh
- tests/scripts/setup_test_env.sh", "notes": "Created comprehensive E2E test infrastructure:
- tests/e2e/mod.rs, framework.rs, logging.rs - Rust test modules
- 7 config fixtures in tests/e2e/fixtures/configs/
- 4 command fixtures in tests/e2e/fixtures/commands/
- tests/scripts/run_e2e_tests.sh - Master E2E runner
- tests/scripts/setup_test_env.sh - Environment setup
All files syntactically correct.  {"id": "git_safety_guard-v47", "title": "[PACK] secrets.aws_secrets - AWS Secrets Manager Pack", "description": "# AWS Secrets Manager Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS Secrets Manager and Parameter Store operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Secrets Manager
- `aws secretsmanager delete-secret`
- `aws secretsmanager delete-resource-policy`
- `aws secretsmanager remove-regions-from-replication`

### Parameter Store (SSM)
- `aws ssm delete-parameter`
- `aws ssm delete-parameters`

## Safe Patterns
- `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`
- `aws secretsmanager describe-secret`
- `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value`
- `aws ssm get-parameter`, `get-parameters`
- `aws ssm describe-parameters`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: aws, secretsmanager, ssm
- Note: delete-secret has recovery window by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Secrets Manager patterns covered
- [ ] Parameter Store patterns covered
- [ ] Tests verify common operations", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-01-09T19:31:01.807224465Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-01-10T22:34:47.819316228Z", "closed_at": "2026-01-10T22:34:47.819316228Z", "close_reason": "Completed: added secrets.aws_secrets pack + tests and registered in pack registry.
